items.briefing:
Australian organisations should be aware that while overall cybercrime risk for Australians has reportedly declined, pressure is increasingly shifting onto small and medium businesses. Ransomware groups are now impersonating Interpol in emails targeting small businesses — if your organisation receives an unexpected message claiming to be from an international law enforcement agency, treat it with suspicion. Separately, Australia has replaced its Essential Eight cybersecurity framework with a new "Essentials" standard, so organisations should review whether their current security practices need updating to remain compliant.

The number of critical software vulnerabilities globally has doubled, meaning unpatched systems are at greater risk than ever. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team or provider that all software and devices are up to date — this remains your most effective defence against current threats.

featured.title: Australia replaces Essential Eight with new Essentials
featured.quote: Australia's cyber baseline shift to cloud-aligned standards will reshape security requirements for all businesses.
